seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in AutoGen Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in AutoGen agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AutoGen, sensitive data leakage in AI agents occurs when agents inadvertently expose confidential information like API keys, user credentials, or PII during multi-agent conversations, tool calls, or code execution. This manifests uniquely due to AutoGen's dynamic message passing between agents and its support for code execution environments where agents can generate and run code containing secrets. Without proper safeguards, agents may propagate leaked data across conversation threads, amplifying exposure risks.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent shares config with embedded API keys in group chat
howItManifests.symptom: Messages contain patterns like 'sk-...' or 'Bearer ...' tokens
howItManifests.impact: All agents in conversation receive and potentially log sensitive credentials

howItManifests.scenario: Code execution agent generates scripts with hardcoded secrets
howItManifests.symptom: Executed code blocks contain database URLs or private keys
howItManifests.impact: Secrets persist in execution environment and conversation history

howItManifests.scenario: Human-in-loop agent receives PII from upstream agents
howItManifests.symptom: Messages include email addresses, phone numbers, or SSNs
howItManifests.impact: Human operators exposed to unredacted personal data

howItManifests.scenario: Tool calling agent embeds secrets in function parameters
howItManifests.symptom: Tool call JSON payloads contain authentication tokens
howItManifests.impact: External services receive leaked credentials via agent tools

detection.name: Regex Pattern Scanning in Agent Messages
detection.description: Scans AutoGen message content for common secret patterns before forwarding

detection.name: AutoGen Reply Function Secret Filter
detection.description: Intercepts agent replies using AutoGen's reply function mechanism
detection.code:
def secret_filter_reply_function(messages, sender, config):
    for msg in messages[-3:]:  # Check recent messages
        detection = detect_secrets_in_message(msg)
        if detection:
            print(f'SECRET DETECTED: {detection}')
            return 'BLOCKED: Sensitive data detected in message'
    return None  # Allow message

# Register with AutoGen agent
agent.register_reply([secret_filter_reply_function], reply_func_args=dict(num_agents=2))

detection.name: Code Execution Environment Secret Scanner
detection.description: Monitors code execution outputs for embedded secrets

fixes.name: Message Redaction Middleware
fixes.description: Automatically redacts sensitive patterns in AutoGen messages

fixes.name: Environment Variable Injection for Code Agents
fixes.description: Replaces hardcoded secrets with environment variables in generated code
fixes.code:
import os
import re

def inject_env_vars(code):
    env_mapping = {
        r'"?sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{48}"?' : 'os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")',
        r'"?your-api-key-here"?' : 'os.getenv("API_KEY")',
        r'"postgres://[^\s]+"' : 'os.getenv("DATABASE_URL")'
    }
    for pattern, replacement in env_mapping.items():
        code = re.sub(pattern, replacement, code)
    return code

# In code execution agent
safe_code = inject_env_vars(generated_code)
result = execute_code(safe_code)

fixes.name: PII Masking with Presidio Analyzer
fixes.description: Uses Microsoft's Presidio for comprehensive PII detection and redaction
fixes.code:
from presidio_analyzer import AnalyzerEngine
from presidio_anonymizer import AnonymizerEngine

def mask_pii_with_presidio(text):
    analyzer = AnalyzerEngine()
    results = analyzer.analyze(text=text, language='en')
    anonymizer = AnonymizerEngine()
    anonymized_text = anonymizer.anonymize(text=text, analyzer_results=results)
    return anonymized_text.text

def presidio_reply_filter(messages, sender, config):
    for msg in messages:
        safe_content = mask_pii_with_presidio(msg.get('content', ''))
        if safe_content != msg.get('content', ''):
            msg['content'] = safe_content
            print('PII redacted with Presidio')
    return None

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade automation for sensitive data leakage prevention in AutoGen by registering as a reply function filter that intercepts all agent communications. It employs advanced regex patterns, ML-based secret detection, and contextual analysis to identify API keys, PII, and credentials across message content, code blocks, and tool calls. Vex automatically redacts detected secrets, blocks risky messages, and generates remediation reports with line-level precision.

The integration requires only `pip install autogen vex-sdk` followed by a single registration call. Vex maintains full conversation context across multi-agent threads, preventing secret propagation between specialized agents. It also monitors code execution environments, ensuring generated scripts don't embed credentials before execution.

Vex provides comprehensive observability with dashboards showing leakage patterns, agent-specific risks, and remediation effectiveness. Teams can configure custom secret patterns and integrate with enterprise secret management systems for automatic remediation workflows.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent

# Install: pip install autogen vex-sdk

# Initialize Vex for AutoGen
vex_client = vex.VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

# Register Vex as reply function filter
def vex_secrets_filter(messages, sender, config):
    verdict = vex_client.scan_autogen_messages(messages)
    if verdict['risk_score'] > 0.7:
        vex_client.report_leakage(
            agent_id=sender,
            messages=messages,
            risk_type='sensitive_data'
        )
        return f"[VEX BLOCKED] Risk score: {verdict['risk_score']:.2f}. Secrets redacted."
    return None

# Create agents with Vex protection
llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4", "api_key": os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")}]}

coder = AssistantAgent(
    name="coder",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    reply_function=[vex_secrets_filter]
)

user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    name="user_proxy",
    code_execution_config=False,
    reply_function=[vex_secrets_filter]
)

# Vex monitors all conversations automatically
user_proxy.initiate_chat(coder, message="Write code using my API key: sk-abc123")

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK and register as a reply filter to automatically prevent sensitive data leakage across all AutoGen conversations.
